text:
### Artist Statement

I am Xingyu Lai, an interdisciplinary artist from China, currently pursuing my BFA at the School of the Art Institute of Chicago. My art practice is deeply rooted in painting, which serves as the foundation for my work, but it has organically expanded into sculpture, ceramics, photography, and interactive installations over time. I focus on the subtle habits of the body, exploring how innate actions—breathing, nursing, and circulating blood—connect individuals to one another and to the systems they inhabit. These small, often overlooked behaviors shape perception, emotion, and psychology in profound ways. Centered on the human experience, my work investigates the relationships between the body, identity, and everyday life. My work aims to reveal these invisible links, allowing viewers to sense the presence and significance of the body through observation or participation.
